headline: Market Recap: Fed Independence Upheld, AI Memory Bottlenecks Persist, China's Economy Gains
summary: The Supreme Court affirmed the Federal Reserve's independence by blocking a presidential attempt to remove a governor. Memory prices are projected to surge with HBM and DRAM identified as critical AI bottlenecks. China's economy showed signs of recovery in June, driven by rebounding U.S. exports.

bullets.lead: Trump-era tariffs are not yielding expected job benefits in the US.

segments.text: Despite tariffs, Whirlpool's Iowa refrigerator plant has not seen job growth, indicating the policy's limited impact on domestic employment.

sources.title: Trump’s tariffs aren’t saving jobs at Whirlpool’s Iowa refrigerator plant

bullets.lead: The EU is engaging China in trade talks over a substantial deficit.

segments.text: The European Union has initiated three months of discussions with China to address a €360 billion trade deficit.

sources.title: EU sets up three months of talks with China over €360bn trade deficit

bullets.lead: China is advancing its domestic memory chip industry.

segments.text: Chinese memory makers are intensifying efforts to boost local chip equipment, with CXMT securing a $3 billion deal from Tencent for memory chip supply.

sources.title: China memory makers fuel local chip equipment push as CXMT lands Tencent deal
Tencent Get’s $3 Billion Worth of Memory Chip Supply from China’s CXMT

bullets.lead: China's AI models are demonstrating advanced cybersecurity capabilities.

segments.text: China's Zhipu AI has reportedly achieved cybersecurity benchmarks comparable to Anthropic's Mythos, raising concerns about intellectual property security.

bullets.lead: The Supreme Court has upheld the Federal Reserve's independence.

segments.text: The Supreme Court blocked former President Trump's attempt to remove Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, reinforcing the Fed's autonomy from political interference.

sources.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook
📹 What the Supreme Court’s Ruling Over Lisa Cook Means for the Fed
2 Supreme Court rulings treat the Fed as an exception

bullets.lead: Bank of America anticipates a reversal of previous Fed rate actions.

segments.text: Bank of America analysts suggest the Federal Reserve will likely withdraw its interest rate cuts from the previous year.

sources.title: [부자들의 투자노트] BofA "연준, 지난해 금리인하 조치 철회할 것"

bullets.lead: Commodity prices are reacting to geopolitical tensions and a stronger dollar.

segments.text: Gold and copper prices declined amid renewed US-Iran tensions, which fueled inflation concerns, and the strengthening US dollar.

sources.title: Gold Declines as Fresh US-Iran Tension Fans Inflation Concerns
Gold prices today, Monday, June 29: Holding at last week's levels ahead of June jobs report

bullets.lead: US oil reserves have fallen to a multi-decade low.

segments.text: US Strategic Petroleum Reserve stocks have decreased by 5.5 million barrels, reaching their lowest level since 1983.

sources.title: Oil stocks in US Strategic Petroleum Reserve fall by 5.5 million to lowest level since 1983

bullets.lead: HBM and DRAM are identified as key bottlenecks for AI development.

segments.text: Industry experts, including Elon Musk, highlight DRAM and HBM capacity as the most critical constraints for AI, with Musk reportedly prioritizing memory for Terafab development.

bullets.lead: Memory prices are projected to surge significantly through 2028.

segments.text: Jefferies forecasts a substantial increase in memory prices, with a 50% rise in Q3 2026 and another 40% in Q4, expecting sustained high prices until 2028 due to AI demand.

bullets.lead: TSMC is expanding its collaborations for advanced memory and optical components.

segments.text: TSMC is partnering with Winbond Electronics on DRAM memory wafers and Wafer-on-Wafer (WoW) 3D stacking technology, and with ShunSin Technology for Fiber Array Unit and Grating Coupling within its COUPE optical engine platform.

bullets.lead: South Korean memory and foundry giants plan massive capex.

segments.text: SK Hynix and Samsung are reportedly planning $1.3 trillion in semiconductor investments over the next decade, doubling prior estimates.

bullets.lead: Microsoft is facing its worst monthly performance in decades.

segments.text: Microsoft's stock experienced a $570 billion rout, positioning it for its most significant monthly decline since 2000.

sources.title: Microsoft’s $570 Billion Rout Sets Up Its Worst Month Since 2000

bullets.lead: Amazon is considering increased payments for Anthropic's AI technology.

segments.text: Amazon may agree to pay more for Anthropic's AI technology under a potential new agreement.

sources.title: Amazon Could Pay More for Anthropic Technology Under New Deal

bullets.lead: Meta is implementing internal restrictions on certain AI models.

segments.text: Internal documents reveal Meta is imposing limits on its use of Claude and Codex models, driven by concerns over potential model distillation.

sources.title: Internal Docs Show Meta Putting Limits on Claude and Codex, Fearing Distillation

bullets.lead: Maersk has revised its profit outlook upwards.

segments.text: Maersk increased its profit guidance, citing new US tariffs as a factor boosting demand.

sources.title: Maersk raises profit guidance as new US tariffs fuel demand

bullets.lead: HD Hyundai Construction Equipment is poised for a stock rebound.

segments.text: KB Securities anticipates a stock rebound for HD Hyundai Construction Equipment, supported by favorable industry conditions and strong earnings performance.

sources.title: KB증권 "HD건설기계, 양호한 업황·실적…주가 반등 기대"

bullets.lead: Nvidia's stock rallied following strong guidance.

segments.text: Nvidia shares saw a rally after the company reported a guidance beat.

sources.title: China's economy picks up in June on rebounding U.S. exports, analysts say

x_themes.topic: Memory Price Surge Forecast Amid AI Data Center Boom
x_themes.summary: Jefferies forecasts a significant surge in memory prices, with a 50% increase in Q3 2026 and another 40% in Q4, expecting no relief until 2028, as AI sales are seen justifying the data center spending boom.

x_themes.topic: HBM/DRAM as Primary Bottleneck for LLM Inference
x_themes.summary: The adoption of open-source LLMs by enterprises is accelerating, with quantization reducing TCO for self-hosted inference; however, HBM capacity and DRAM remain the primary constraints and durable demand drivers for the GAI hardware stack.

TSMC is partnering with Winbond Electronics for DRAM memory wafers and Wafer-on-Wafer (WoW) advanced 3D stacking technology, diversifying its memory sourcing beyond SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron for AI chips.
SK Hynix and Samsung are reportedly planning $1.3 trillion in spending over the next decade, doubling previous estimates, indicating significant investment in their semiconductor operations.

x_themes.topic: DRAM as the Critical Bottleneck for AI, Elon's Focus
x_themes.summary: Industry experts, including Elon Musk, emphasize DRAM as the most critical bottleneck for AI, with Musk reportedly prioritizing memory for Terafab development, validating its strategic importance.

x_themes.topic: SGLang vs. vLLM Performance for GLM-5.2 NVFP4 Inference
x_themes.summary: A benchmark of GLM-5.2 NVFP4 inference engines shows SGLang outperforming vLLM for 32K-256K context workloads in speed and latency, while vLLM demonstrates superior stability for 512K/768K long-context tasks due to its CPU KV-offload capability.

x_themes.topic: China's AI Efficiency & Open-Source Model Threat
x_themes.summary: China is seen potentially winning the AI race by generating more intelligence per dollar of capex, with competitive open-source models like GLM-5.2 matching US counterparts at a fraction of the cost, leading to US hyperscalers cutting AI spending and concerns about IP theft.

x_themes.topic: China's Zhipu AI Matches Anthropic in Cybersecurity
x_themes.summary: China's Zhipu AI has reportedly matched Anthropic's Mythos in cybersecurity benchmarks, raising concerns about IP theft and the need for stronger guardrails to prevent model distillation, effectively resetting aspects of the AI race.

x_themes.topic: Rapid Growth & Investment in Robotics Sector
x_themes.summary: The robotics sector is experiencing rapid growth, with deal values expected to increase tenfold, attracting significant private capital into companies like Saronic, Skild AI, and Apptronik, and creating public investment opportunities in upstream suppliers and pure-play robotics firms.

x_themes.topic: South Korea's Crucial Role in Humanoid Robotics Supply Chain
x_themes.summary: South Korea is projected to supply 30% of humanoid components by 2035, leveraging its automotive industry expertise to become a leading non-Chinese supplier of critical actuators, which constitute about 50% of a humanoid's BOM.

x_themes.topic: Automotive Suppliers' Role in Humanoid Robotics BOM
x_themes.summary: Automotive suppliers like Schaeffler are becoming crucial for humanoid robotics, covering up to 50% of a humanoid's Bill of Materials with components like actuators and sensors, with humanoids and AI auto expected to be a significant catalyst post-2027.
A past interview with Ouster's CEO is highlighted as a valuable resource for understanding the company's lidar business, its revenue generation, and long-term vision.
Lumentum's EML roadmap may transition from 200G to 300G before 400G, potentially altering demand timing, technology transition pace, and Lumentum's competitive standing in the optical market.

x_themes.topic: TSMC-ShunSin Collaboration on COUPE Optical Engine
x_themes.summary: TSMC is partnering with ShunSin Technology for Fiber Array Unit (FAU) and Grating Coupling work within its COUPE optical engine platform, with ShunSin reporting strong growth, capex plans, and advancements in CPO production.
